questions.question: Why was the traditional property requirement for military service considered philosophically important to Romans? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: It ensured only the educated classes could serve in the military
Men who owned land had something worth fighting for
The system created a natural check on military adventurism since soldiers wanted to return home
It prevented foreigners from infiltrating the legions
Soldiers who served alongside neighbors had natural unit cohesion

questions.question: What pressures were causing the traditional Roman military system to collapse by the late second century BCE? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: Germanic invasions had destroyed most of Italy's farmland
Rome's empire required permanent garrisons that made farming impossible
The eligible soldier pool was shrinking while military obligations expanded
Economic transformation had displaced tens of thousands of small farmers
Men who had lost their property were legally prohibited from service

questions.question: Why did the Jugurthine War become a political scandal in Rome? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: The war showcased aristocratic corruption and incompetence
Marius was accused of prolonging the war for personal profit
Roman commanders repeatedly accepted bribes from Jugurtha
The historian Sallust claimed Jugurtha called Rome 'a city for sale'
The Senate secretly supported Jugurtha against Roman interests

questions.question: How did Marius obtain command of the Numidian War? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: He was appointed by the Senate as a reward for his service under Metellus
The popular assembly used a tribune's legislation to assign him the command
He won the consulship on an anti-aristocratic platform
He bribed his way into the command using wealth from Spanish mines
This overrode the Senate's traditional prerogative to assign military commands

questions.question: How did the new professional soldiers differ from the old citizen-soldiers? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: They had a direct stake in the existing order through property ownership
They had no farms to return to after service
Their future depended entirely on their general's political success
They swore loyalty oaths directly to the Senate
The legion became their home, community, and family

questions.question: Why did the defeat at Arausio cause such panic in Rome? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: It proved that Marius's military reforms had failed
The disaster was comparable to Cannae in scale
It awakened ancient fears dating to the Gallic sack of 390 BCE
The road to Italy appeared to lay open to invasion
The entire Senate was captured by the Germanic tribes

questions.question: What did Marius do during the years 104-102 BCE while waiting for the Germanic tribes? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: He immediately pursued the Germans into Spain
He trained his army relentlessly in southern Gaul
He kept soldiers busy with engineering projects
He was elected consul repeatedly as Rome clung to its savior
He negotiated a peace treaty with the Cimbri

questions.question: What problems did the issue of veterans' welfare create after Marius's reforms? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: The Senate automatically provided generous pensions to all soldiers
Professional soldiers had nothing to return to after service
Generals needed to provide land for veterans or face mutiny
Land distribution required legislation through the Senate and assemblies
Veterans' welfare made generals into political actors

questions.question: What happened during the Saturninus crisis of 100 BCE? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: Saturninus and Glaucia's followers murdered a consular candidate
Marius refused to act and was exiled from Rome
The Senate passed the senatus consultum ultimum
Marius besieged his former allies on the Capitoline Hill
Saturninus successfully established himself as dictator

questions.question: Why did the Saturninus affair damage Marius's political position? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: He was hailed as a hero for restoring constitutional order
The populares felt he had betrayed his allies
The Senate distrusted him as a popular demagogue
He was formally expelled from the Senate
He left Rome and had no major command for years
